summary_one_line: A multi-glyph Indic syllable whose base consonant ends with indic_position above POS_BASE_C, reached via a deep reordering call context.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input decoded into an Indic syllable with at least 2 glyphs where, after base-finding, the base glyph's indic_position() exceeds POS_BASE_C and start < base — i.e. multi-consonant clusters that drive the final_reordering machinery past the simple-base case into the matra/reph reordering paths.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seeds are dense repeated-byte glyph runs (e.g. 22-byte seed of repeated 0x75 'u' plus 0x97 runs) that decode to long uniform syllables, exercising the deep reordering paths; naive_ctx folds the call-context into the edge map, so reaching L1099 via the specific syllable-segmentation/base-finding call path registers as NEW coverage and is retained, repeatedly steering mutation toward the rare base>POS_BASE_C configuration (winner resolved=10/10).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser (naive) sees only the bare edge at L1099, which it already covers on the False side (T=0 F=17 in one variant), so the rare True side earns no novelty credit; its blocking seeds are sparse 0x09/0x0c/0x00 patterns that stay in the simple-base / no-pre-base-matra regime and it blocks 8/10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds a running hash of the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same L1099 edge counts as distinct coverage per call context; this context-sensitive coverage rewards seeds that reach the deep reordering path through new call contexts, driving naive_ctx to retain and extend them while plain naive gives the bare edge no novelty.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive_ctx > naive with delta ctx_coverage, prob_div 0.80.
The winner resolved all 10 trials while the loser blocked 8 of 10.
The blocking branch requires start < base and base's indic_position above POS_BASE_C.
The blocked True side of L1099 is rarely taken; loser shows T=0 on one variant while winner reaches T=2.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1099  T=2 F=0  T=0 F=17  if (start < base && info[base].indic_position() > POS_BAS...  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-resolving seeds are dense repeated-glyph byte runs that decode to long uniform syllables.
Loser-blocking seeds are sparse 0x09/0x0c/0x00 patterns lacking the dense glyph runs.
naive_ctx folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different slots depending on call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: For naive_ctx a new coverage bucket is a new (call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge, unlike naive.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: Plain naive uses a per-edge hit-count bucket with no context indexing, so a covered edge yields no further novelty.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Not applicable: claimed_mechanism is context-sensitive coverage (naive_ctx CtxHook), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory dash-row lineage check does not apply. The naive_ctx MECHANISM CONTEXT explicitly states there are no I2SRandReplace/CMP_MAP mutators and no ParentInfo-only/dash rows, so an I2S-floor signal is not expected for this fuzzer.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on info[base].indic_position() > POS_BASE_C in which naive_ctx and naive resolve the True side at statistically indistinguishable rates — showing the context-indexed edge map provided no advantage at this branch.

weakest_evidence_point: The seed-byte interpretation (dense repeated-glyph runs decode into the multi-consonant syllable that produces base>POS_BASE_C) is inferred from byte patterns rather than from a decoded glyph trace, so the exact glyph-level cause is not directly observed.
